decision_register.summary: Zero. The cache has the schema (`transcript_cache` table exists) but 0 rows. The script has never actually downloaded and cached any transcripts — it's only been run in dry-run mode (no `--run` flag), or any live runs exited before the background writer thread could flush its queue to disk. The Rick Astley analysis that exists (`dQw4w9WgXcQ.json`) used `mode="transcript"` but the `fallback_reason` shows `summarize_timeout_120s` — the summarize step timed out, not the transcript fetch. The transcript fetch itself may have succeeded via one of the free sources (ytdlp, youtube_transcript_api), but it was never cached because `set_cached_transcript` writes to a background queue that needs the process to stay alive long enough to flush.
